title: pedicularis rupicola subsp. zambalensis
intro: Pedicularis rupicola subsp. zambalensis, also known as the Zambal lousewort, is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The Zambal lousewort is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antiseptic and expectorant properties. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are used to create tonics, teas, and other remedies.
